_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.

preauth.label: Clinical Notes including evaluation findings, indications for the procedure, and planned line of treatment

claim.label: Detailed Indoor case papers and treatment given details
Post procedure blood glucose (fasting and PP) report

ppd_preauth.q: Was the Random or casual plasma glucose ≥200 mg/dl or fasting plasma glucose > 126 mg/dl?
Was the ketone level in urine > 3?
Was the serum ketone positive with 1:2 dilution?

cpd_claim.q: Was the Random or casual plasma glucose ≥200 mg/dl or fasting plasma glucose > 126 mg/dl?
Was the ketone level in urine > 3?
Was the serum ketone positive with 1:2 dilution?

common_queries: Plasma glucose level not meeting diagnostic criteria (random/casual < 200 mg/dl or fasting ≤ 126 mg/dl).
Urine ketone level not meeting diagnostic criteria (≤ 3).
Serum ketone test not positive with 1:2 dilution.
Arterial Blood Gas (ABG) report not providing clear evidence of acidosis.
Mandatory Clinical Notes, evaluation findings, indications, or planned line of treatment not submitted at pre-authorization.
Mandatory Sr. Bicarbonate report missing at pre-authorization.
Mandatory Arterial Blood Gas (ABG) report missing at pre-authorization.
Mandatory Urine Ketone report missing at pre-authorization.
Mandatory Detailed Indoor case papers and treatment given details missing at claim submission.
Mandatory Post-procedure blood glucose (fasting and PP) report missing at claim submission.
Mandatory Detailed discharge summary missing at claim submission.

additional_information.min_doctor_qualification_desirable: MD/DNB equivalent (in General Medicine, Pediatric Medicine)
clinical_key_pointers: Proceed with Diabetic ketoacidosis only if diagnosis made is backed by clinical manifestation.
